description_long: This object is a compact submachine gun with a utilitarian, minimalist design, reminiscent of the British Sten gun. It features a tubular metal receiver and barrel, with a prominent side-mounted detachable box magazine. The stock is a simple skeletonized metal rod, folding to the side for portability. The trigger guard and pistol grip are integrated into the main body, and the iron sights are fixed, with a basic post and notch configuration. The overall finish is matte black, with visible welds and rivets, giving it an industrial, rugged appearance.
description: A compact, matte black submachine gun with a side-mounted magazine, skeletonized folding stock, and fixed iron sights.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from above, highlighting the side-mounted magazine and the linear alignment of the barrel and stock.
description_view_1: A full side profile reveals the skeletonized folding stock, side magazine, and the overall slim, utilitarian build.
description_view_2: Viewed from below, the magazine and barrel alignment are visible, with the trigger guard and grip partially obscured.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile shows the magazine well, trigger, and the simple, industrial design of the receiver and stock.
